title: Study bonuses — functional implementation (not flavor text)
problem: The 'Study' action in the Graveyard showed a permanent knowledge bonus description per item quality, but none of these bonuses were actually applied in game logic. Studying an item consumed it for flavor text only. The bonus system needed to be wired into loot display and identification.

lineage.spawnReason: deferred mechanic: study bonuses were deliberately stubbed as flavor text pending integration with loot and identification systems

analysis: The studiedBonuses[] array on careerStats already existed but was only used for flavor descriptions in GraveyardScreen. Four bonus tiers are now mechanically active: (1) unique — auto-identify all unique drops in LootScreen and CombatStore handleVictory; (2) rare — animated gold-pulse banner and tinted row highlighting for rare drops in LootScreen; (3) magic — show unidentified affix count below the item row for magic items; (4) normal — show socket count below the item row for normal items with sockets. These bonuses compound — a player who has studied items of multiple qualities sees multiple UI layers simultaneously.
implementation: combatStore.ts/handleVictory: reads careerStats.studiedBonuses, if includes 'unique' iterates pendingLoot and sets item.identified=true for quality==='unique'. LootScreen.tsx: reads studiedBonuses from careerStats. (1) hasRareDrop = studiedBonuses.includes('rare') && pendingLoot.some(i=>i.quality==='rare') — drives rarePulse Animated.Value (1100ms loop 0.2→0.9) and rareBanner text '◈ RARE SIGNAL · STUDIED KNOWLEDGE'. (2) rareRow style: gold tint background+border applied to rare items when bonus active. (3) studyHint text below ItemCard: normal items show '⬡ N socket(s)' if sockets>0 when 'normal' bonus active; magic items show '◈ N affixes inside' if unidentified + affixes>0 when 'magic' bonus active. Frailty pact also triggers auto-identify (all qualities) — these two mechanics compose correctly since both just set item.identified=true.

decisions.decision: Study bonuses are applied at loot display time, not at drop generation
decisions.rationale: Applying auto-identify at display time (LootScreen + handleVictory) means the bonus retroactively applies to any item in pendingLoot without needing to thread the studiedBonuses array through rollLoot(). Simpler call graph, no engine coupling.
alternativesConsidered: Pass studiedBonuses to rollLoot() — adds parameter to pure engine function, breaks the engine/store boundary
decisions.recommendation: Apply bonuses in handleVictory (identify pass) and LootScreen (UI hints). Engine stays pure.

decisions.decision: Rare bonus is visual-only (highlight + pulse), not an identify bonus
decisions.rationale: Rare items are always identifiable by the player already (tap to open sheet). The rare bonus delivers value by making rare drops unmissable — the gold pulse draws the eye before the player even reads the item names. This is higher UX value than auto-identification for rares.
alternativesConsidered: Auto-identify rares — makes the bonus feel similar to the unique bonus without distinguishing the two
decisions.recommendation: Animated pulse banner + row tint for rares. Unique bonus handles identification. Stack-agnostic: both are pure UI state.

successCriteria: studiedBonuses.includes('unique') → all unique drops auto-identified in handleVictory. studiedBonuses.includes('rare') → rareBanner pulses and rare rows are gold-tinted in LootScreen. studiedBonuses.includes('magic') → affix count hint shown for unidentified magic drops. studiedBonuses.includes('normal') → socket count hint shown for socketed normal drops. All four bonuses compose without conflict. TypeScript 0 errors.
knownLimitations: studiedBonuses[] stores quality strings not per-item UIDs — if a player studies two magic items the bonus activates after the first; there is no 'rarer knowledge from more studies' progression
The 'rare' bonus highlight does not persist to InventoryScreen — items are uncolored once in bag
No bonus for runeword quality (studying a runeword base is undefined behavior)
userGuide:
## Study Bonuses — Active Effects

When you **Study** a graveyard item, you gain permanent knowledge keyed to its quality. After studying, the bonus is active on every future run:

- **Normal item studied** — Socket info revealed on loot drops: a hint like `⬡ 2 sockets` appears under normal items on the loot screen, so you can spot runeword candidates without opening them.
- **Magic item studied** — Affix count revealed: unidentified magic items show `◈ 3 affixes inside`, telling you if it's worth identifying before picking up.
- **Rare item studied** — Rare drops pulse gold on the loot screen (`◈ RARE SIGNAL · STUDIED KNOWLEDGE` banner) and receive a gold-tinted highlight row. You will never miss a rare drop again.
- **Unique item studied** — All unique drops are automatically identified when they land on the loot screen. No need to tap to identify.

Bonuses stack: studying one item of each quality gives you all four effects simultaneously.

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Full mechanical implementation of study bonuses — wired to LootScreen UI and handleVictory auto-identify

publicInterface.name: CareerStats.studiedBonuses
publicInterface.description: Quality IDs ('normal'|'magic'|'rare'|'unique') of studied graveyard entries. Each entry activates the corresponding loot bonus permanently.

codeSnippets.label: Auto-identify uniques in handleVictory (combatStore)
Rare pulse animation in LootScreen
